symptom_description: Bed temperature exceeding 950°C — risk of clinker formation
probable_causes: 1. Excess air reduction|2. High calorific value fuel|3. Air distribution plate blockage|4. Thermocouple error
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check combustion air ratio — target 1.2-1.3|2. Analyze fuel sample for CV|3. Inspect air nozzles for blockage|4. Cross-check bed TC with portable pyrometer
corrective_actions: 1. Increase PA air to improve fluidization|2. Blend fuel to reduce CV|3. Clean blocked air nozzles|4. Recalibrate thermocouples
preventive_actions: 1. Regular fuel quality testing|2. Scheduled air nozzle inspection every 500 hrs|3. TC calibration every 3 months
spare_parts_needed: Air nozzles,Thermocouples,Bed material

symptom_description: Boiler efficiency dropped below 82% — high fuel consumption
probable_causes: 1. Air preheater fouling|2. High unburnt carbon in fly ash|3. Tube surface fouling|4. Air ingress in convection pass
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check APH gas-side ΔT|2. Analyze fly ash LOI — target < 3%|3. Check flue gas O2 — target 3-4%|4. Inspect for air ingress at expansion joints
corrective_actions: 1. Clean APH tubes — chemical or mechanical|2. Adjust bed height and PA/SA ratio|3. Schedule soot blowing|4. Seal air ingress points
preventive_actions: 1. APH cleaning every 2000 hrs|2. Continuous flue gas monitoring|3. Regular soot blowing schedule
spare_parts_needed: APH cleaning chemicals,Soot blower lances,Sealing materials

symptom_description: Water/steam leak detected — wet spots on casing or unusual noise
probable_causes: 1. Erosion at soot blower impingement zone|2. Corrosion — fireside or waterside|3. Thermal fatigue at bends|4. Manufacturing defect
diagnostic_steps: 1. Acoustic leak detection to locate|2. Shutdown and visual inspection|3. UT thickness measurement at suspected areas|4. Metallurgical analysis of failed tube
corrective_actions: 1. Emergency shutdown per SOP-BLR-03|2. Cut and replace affected tube section|3. Hydrotest after repair|4. NDT inspection of adjacent tubes
preventive_actions: 1. Regular UT thickness surveys every 6 months|2. Water chemistry monitoring — pH 8.5-9.5|3. Soot blower alignment check
spare_parts_needed: Water wall tubes,Welding consumables,UT probe

symptom_description: Flame flickering or extinguishing — burner not holding stable flame
probable_causes: 1. Fuel nozzle blockage or wear|2. Combustion air damper misadjusted|3. Fuel pressure fluctuation|4. Flame scanner dirty or misaligned
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect fuel nozzle spray pattern|2. Check combustion air damper position vs setpoint|3. Verify fuel pressure at burner — steady ± 0.5 bar|4. Clean flame scanner lens and check alignment
corrective_actions: 1. Clean or replace fuel nozzle|2. Recalibrate air damper per SOP-TF-03|3. Check fuel pump and strainer|4. Clean flame scanner
preventive_actions: 1. Nozzle inspection every 1000 hrs|2. Combustion tuning quarterly|3. Fuel strainer cleaning weekly
spare_parts_needed: Fuel nozzle assembly,Air damper actuator,Flame scanner

symptom_description: Stack temperature above 250°C — indicating heat loss
probable_causes: 1. Fouling on heating coils|2. Excess air too high|3. Thermic fluid degradation — reduced heat transfer|4. Incorrect air-fuel ratio
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check TF inlet-outlet ΔT vs design|2. Measure O2 in flue gas — should be 3-4%|3. Test TF fluid properties — flash point and viscosity|4. Inspect coil surface for carbon deposits
corrective_actions: 1. Clean heating coils if fouled|2. Adjust combustion air to reduce O2|3. Partial or full fluid replacement if degraded|4. Descale coils if carbon buildup
preventive_actions: 1. Fluid quality testing every 6 months|2. Combustion analysis monthly|3. Coil inspection during annual shutdown
spare_parts_needed: TF fluid (partial charge),Cleaning chemicals,O2 analyzer probe

symptom_description: Drum level swinging ±100mm — affecting steam quality
probable_causes: 1. Feedwater control valve hunting|2. Sudden load changes from GT|3. 3-element control tuning issue|4. Level transmitter drift
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check control valve response — should be smooth|2. Trend GT load vs drum level — correlation analysis|3. Review PID parameters of FW control loop|4. Cross-check level transmitter with gauge glass
corrective_actions: 1. Retune feedwater control PID|2. Adjust control valve positioner|3. Recalibrate level transmitter|4. Consider cascade control implementation
preventive_actions: 1. Control loop tuning review every quarter|2. Level transmitter calibration every 6 months|3. Valve positioner maintenance annually
spare_parts_needed: Control valve trim,Level transmitter,Positioner

symptom_description: SO2 emission exceeding 200 mg/Nm3 at stack — non-compliance risk
probable_causes: 1. Low limestone slurry concentration|2. Recirculation pump underperformance|3. Mist eliminator blockage reducing gas contact|4. pH drift in absorber — below 5.5
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check slurry concentration — target 25%|2. Measure pump flow rate vs design|3. Inspect ME for scaling — check ΔP|4. Monitor absorber pH continuously
corrective_actions: 1. Increase limestone feed rate|2. Service pump — check impeller wear|3. Initiate ME wash cycle|4. Adjust pH setpoint to 5.8
preventive_actions: 1. Continuous pH monitoring with alarm|2. Weekly ME wash schedule|3. Pump vibration monitoring|4. Limestone quality testing
spare_parts_needed: Pump impeller,ME blades,pH sensor,Limestone

symptom_description: Chilled water outlet 12°C vs design 7°C — capacity loss
probable_causes: 1. Crystallization in absorber|2. Non-condensable gas accumulation|3. Cooling water temperature too high|4. Weak LiBr solution concentration
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check absorber sump temperature and crystal check|2. Purge non-condensables from system|3. Verify CW inlet temperature and flow|4. Measure LiBr concentration — target 62-64%
corrective_actions: 1. Run decrystallization procedure if crystallized|2. Evacuate and recharge solution|3. Clean CT fills if CW temp high|4. Adjust solution concentration
preventive_actions: 1. Regular vacuum monitoring|2. CW treatment program|3. Solution analysis quarterly|4. CT maintenance schedule
spare_parts_needed: Vacuum pump seals,LiBr solution,CW treatment chemicals

symptom_description: ID fan vibration exceeding 7 mm/s — approaching trip setpoint
probable_causes: 1. Impeller buildup — ash/dust accumulation|2. Bearing wear or misalignment|3. Foundation looseness|4. Coupling misalignment
diagnostic_steps: 1. Measure vibration spectrum — identify 1x or 2x patterns|2. Check bearing temperature trend|3. Inspect foundation bolts torque|4. Check coupling alignment with laser
corrective_actions: 1. Clean impeller — online or during shutdown|2. Replace bearings if needed|3. Retorque foundation bolts|4. Realign coupling per specifications
preventive_actions: 1. Online water washing schedule for impeller|2. Bearing condition monitoring — vibration trending|3. Alignment check during every shutdown
spare_parts_needed: Fan bearings,Coupling elements,Balancing weights

symptom_description: Large clinker lumps forming in bed — affecting fluidization
probable_causes: 1. Bed temperature too high — above 950°C|2. Low-ash-fusion-temperature fuel|3. Insufficient bed drain|4. Air distribution plate damage
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check bed temperature profile — all zones|2. Test fuel ash fusion temperature|3. Inspect bed drain rate — should be continuous|4. Check air distribution plate pressure drop
corrective_actions: 1. Emergency: reduce load and cool bed|2. Increase bed drain rate|3. Blend with higher AFT fuel|4. Inspect and replace damaged nozzles
preventive_actions: 1. Fuel AFT testing for every new lot|2. Bed temperature monitoring with multiple TCs|3. Regular bed drain and replenishment
spare_parts_needed: Bed material (fresh),Air nozzles,Refractory patches

symptom_description: Cyclone separator refractory wear — particle leakage to convection pass
probable_causes: 1. High circulating material velocity|2. Abrasive fuel ash composition|3. Incorrect refractory material selection|4. Overloading beyond design capacity
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect cyclone vortex finder and barrel refractory|2. Measure refractory thickness at wear points|3. Analyze ash abrasion index|4. Check circulating load — bed pressure drop trend
corrective_actions: 1. Replace refractory with SiC-based castable|2. Reduce circulating material velocity|3. Install ceramic tiles at high-wear zones|4. Adjust cyclone geometry if feasible
preventive_actions: 1. Refractory thickness survey every shutdown|2. Fuel ash abrasion testing|3. Cyclone DP monitoring for early warning
spare_parts_needed: SiC refractory,Ceramic tiles,Vortex finder assembly

symptom_description: Bed pressure fluctuating ±500 mmWC — unstable fluidization regime
probable_causes: 1. Air distribution nozzle blockage|2. Wet or oversized fuel|3. PA fan output variation|4. Bed material particle size out of range
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check PA fan discharge pressure stability|2. Inspect fuel size — should be < 10mm|3. Monitor air nozzle ΔP — uniform across plate|4. Sieve analysis of bed material (target 0.5-1.5mm)
corrective_actions: 1. Clean blocked nozzles|2. Crush or reject oversized fuel|3. Stabilize PA fan with VFD tuning|4. Replace bed material if PSD out of spec
preventive_actions: 1. Regular nozzle inspection every 500 hrs|2. Fuel sizing quality control|3. Bed material PSD monitoring monthly
spare_parts_needed: Air nozzles,Bed material,PA fan VFD card

symptom_description: Superheater tube metal temperature exceeding design limit — creep risk
probable_causes: 1. Excessive GT exhaust temperature|2. Steam flow reduction due to downstream blockage|3. Internal tube deposits reducing cooling|4. Thermocouple failure giving false low readings
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check GT exhaust gas temperature trend|2. Verify steam flow through superheater|3. Compare tube metal TCs with portable measurement|4. Check steam drum pressure and flow
corrective_actions: 1. Coordinate with GT operator to limit exhaust temp|2. Clear downstream steam path restrictions|3. Chemical cleaning of tubes if deposits found|4. Replace failed thermocouples
preventive_actions: 1. GT exhaust temperature interlock|2. Regular tube metal temperature monitoring|3. Steam chemistry control to prevent deposits
spare_parts_needed: Superheater tubes,Thermocouples,Welding consumables

symptom_description: Economizer outlet temperature approaching saturation — steaming risk
probable_causes: 1. Reduced feedwater flow|2. High gas temperature at economizer inlet|3. Feedwater control valve malfunction|4. Recirculation line blocked
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check feedwater flow rate vs design|2. Monitor economizer gas-side inlet temperature|3. Inspect feedwater control valve position|4. Verify recirculation line valve position
corrective_actions: 1. Restore feedwater flow to design rate|2. Open economizer bypass if gas temp high|3. Repair feedwater control valve|4. Open recirculation valve to prevent steaming
preventive_actions: 1. Feedwater flow low alarm|2. Economizer outlet temperature high alarm|3. Regular valve maintenance
spare_parts_needed: Feedwater control valve,Temperature sensors,Recirculation valve

symptom_description: Thermic fluid flash point below 150°C — fire hazard risk
probable_causes: 1. Continuous overtemperature operation|2. Air ingress from expansion tank|3. Water contamination|4. Operating beyond fluid maximum bulk temperature
diagnostic_steps: 1. Lab analysis — flash point/viscosity/carbon residue|2. Check expansion tank nitrogen blanket if applicable|3. Check for water contamination — crackle test|4. Review temperature logs for overtemperature events
corrective_actions: 1. Partial or full fluid replacement|2. Install nitrogen blanketing on expansion tank|3. Fix water leak source if contaminated|4. Reset high-temperature alarm and interlock
preventive_actions: 1. Fluid testing every 6 months|2. Expansion tank inspection annually|3. Temperature interlock verification quarterly
spare_parts_needed: Thermic fluid,Nitrogen supply,Expansion tank gaskets

symptom_description: TF circulation pump trip — heater emergency shutdown activated
probable_causes: 1. Mechanical seal failure|2. Motor overload due to high fluid viscosity|3. Cavitation from low expansion tank level|4. Electrical contactor failure
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check pump mechanical seal — visible leak|2. Monitor motor current vs rated|3. Verify expansion tank level and NPSH|4. Check electrical supply and contactor condition
corrective_actions: 1. Replace mechanical seal|2. Check fluid viscosity — may need partial replacement|3. Top up expansion tank|4. Replace contactor if faulty
preventive_actions: 1. Seal inspection every 3 months|2. Fluid viscosity monitoring|3. Expansion tank level alarm|4. Motor overload relay calibration
spare_parts_needed: Mechanical seal,Contactor,Thermic fluid

symptom_description: Gypsum moisture >15% and purity <90% — not saleable
probable_causes: 1. Hydrocyclone underperformance|2. Vacuum belt filter vacuum loss|3. Oxidation air blower underperformance|4. Limestone impurities
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check hydrocyclone underflow concentration|2. Verify vacuum level on belt filter|3. Measure oxidation air flow rate|4. Test limestone purity — target >95% CaCO3
corrective_actions: 1. Clean hydrocyclone apex nozzle|2. Repair vacuum pump or belt seal|3. Service oxidation air blower|4. Source higher purity limestone

symptom_description: Excessive foaming in FGD absorber — slurry overflow risk
probable_causes: 1. Organic contamination from fuel|2. Surfactant in makeup water|3. High suspended solids in recirculation|4. Chemical dosing error
diagnostic_steps: 1. Visual inspection of foam — color and consistency|2. Test recirculation slurry for organics|3. Check makeup water quality|4. Review chemical dosing logs
corrective_actions: 1. Add antifoam agent|2. Increase blowdown to reduce contaminants|3. Check and adjust chemical dosing|4. Filter makeup water if contaminated
preventive_actions: 1. Makeup water quality monitoring|2. Antifoam dosing system standby|3. Regular blowdown schedule

symptom_description: Stack opacity exceeding 20% — particulate emission non-compliance
probable_causes: 1. ESP field de-energized — T-R set fault|2. Rapping system failure|3. Collecting plate misalignment|4. High-resistivity ash causing back corona
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check T-R set V-I readings — all fields|2. Verify rapping sequence and timing|3. Inspect collecting plate alignment|4. Check ash resistivity — if >10^12 ohm-cm consider conditioning

symptom_description: ESP sparking rate >50 per minute — power input limited
probable_causes: 1. Internal short circuit — broken wire or plate|2. Dust buildup on insulators|3. Excessive dust loading|4. AVC controller malfunction
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check spark rate vs power input curve|2. Inspect insulators for dust/cracks|3. Monitor inlet dust loading|4. Review AVC settings and operation
corrective_actions: 1. Repair broken discharge wire|2. Clean insulators — hot or cold|3. Reduce gas velocity if possible|4. Replace AVC card if faulty

symptom_description: CW outlet temperature 5°C above design — process cooling affected
probable_causes: 1. CT fill media deterioration|2. Fan blade pitch incorrect|3. Algae/scale on fill media|4. Air recirculation due to wind
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect fill media condition — sagging or collapse|2. Check fan blade pitch angle — compare with design|3. Visual inspection for biological growth|4. Check for hot air recirculation pattern
corrective_actions: 1. Replace fill media if deteriorated|2. Adjust fan blade pitch to design angle|3. Clean fill media and dose biocide|4. Install wind walls if recirculation observed

symptom_description: CT fan vibration exceeding 5 mm/s — approaching alarm limit
probable_causes: 1. Fan blade erosion or damage|2. Ice accumulation in winter|3. Gear reducer bearing wear|4. Fan hub bolt looseness
diagnostic_steps: 1. Vibration spectrum analysis — 1x vs blade pass|2. Visual inspection of blade surfaces|3. Check gear reducer oil level and quality|4. Inspect hub bolt torque
corrective_actions: 1. Replace damaged blade|2. De-ice and install anti-icing system|3. Replace gear reducer bearings|4. Retorque hub bolts to specification

symptom_description: LiBr solution crystallized in absorber — chiller inoperative
probable_causes: 1. Cooling water interruption|2. Electric power failure without controlled shutdown|3. Excessive vacuum loss|4. Solution concentration too high
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check sight glass for crystal deposits|2. Measure absorber sump temperature|3. Verify vacuum level|4. Check cooling water supply history
corrective_actions: 1. Initiate decrystallization per SOP-ABS-02|2. Apply hot water to generator 95°C|3. Gradually restore circulation|4. Verify solution concentration after recovery
preventive_actions: 1. CW flow alarm and interlock|2. Auto-dilution system on power failure|3. Vacuum monitoring with alarm|4. Solution concentration check monthly

symptom_description: Generator pressure exceeding 80 kPa — safety relief risk
probable_causes: 1. Fouled generator tubes — reduced heat transfer|2. Non-condensable gas accumulation|3. Cooling water flow to condenser reduced|4. Steam/hot water inlet temperature too high

corrective_actions: 1. Clean generator tubes if fouled|2. Run evacuation pump for non-condensables|3. Restore CW flow to condenser|4. Reduce heat source temperature

symptom_description: Heavy scaling in evaporator effects — capacity drop >30%
probable_causes: 1. Inadequate anti-scalant dosing|2. Feed water hardness above design|3. Operating above concentration limit|4. Insufficient blowdown
diagnostic_steps: 1. Inspect tube surfaces — endoscope or sample tube|2. Scale composition analysis (CaCO3/CaSO4/silica)|3. Check anti-scalant dosing rate vs consumption|4. Verify blowdown schedule

preventive_actions: 1. Feed water hardness monitoring|2. Anti-scalant stock and dosing audit|3. Regular cleaning schedule based on ΔT trending

symptom_description: Evaporator vacuum dropping — product concentration affected
probable_causes: 1. Air leak in system — flange or valve packing|2. Condenser tube fouling|3. Vacuum pump/ejector malfunction|4. Cooling water temperature high

corrective_actions: 1. Tighten flanges or replace gaskets|2. Clean condenser tubes|3. Service vacuum pump — replace seals|4. Restore ejector steam conditions
preventive_actions: 1. Regular leak checks during shutdowns|2. Condenser cleaning schedule|3. Vacuum pump maintenance quarterly

symptom_description: Bag filter DP >200 mmWC — fan power increasing significantly
probable_causes: 1. Bag blinding from moisture or oil|2. Pulse cleaning system failure|3. Excessive dust loading|4. Wrong bag material for application
diagnostic_steps: 1. Check compressed air pressure at pulse valves — target 6 bar|2. Verify pulse timer operation — sequence and interval|3. Check gas inlet temperature vs dew point|4. Inspect bags for blinding — visual or lab test

symptom_description: Multiple bag failures detected — emission spike at stack
probable_causes: 1. Spark or hot gas reaching bags|2. Bag abrasion from cage corrosion|3. Chemical attack — acid or alkali exposure|4. Bag exceeding service life

symptom_description: Flue gas O2 above 6% — indicating excessive air ingress
probable_causes: 1. Air ingress at expansion joints|2. Air ingress at inspection doors|3. Penthouse sealing failure|4. Duct flange leakage
